standard_definition: A measurable observation space and state-indexed family of laws.
One experiment is obtainable from another by an observation-only randomization; equivalently under the theorem conditions it is no better in every decision problem.
Directed distance measuring how closely one experiment can simulate another through randomization.
Prior-averaged expected loss of a decision rule.
Choose a rule minimizing the maximum loss relative to the statewise best action over an ambiguity set.
Observables and assumptions restrict a target to a set rather than a singleton.
Optimize risk against a registered set of distributions or groups.
Maximum conditional expected loss over predeclared groups.
Average upper-tail loss represented by the Rockafellar-Uryasev variational form.
Pure-exploration identification of the action with best mean under arm-wise observations.
c85_mapping: contrast class; not the C85 full-information query

standard_definition: Identify a set containing the best actions under an explicit error criterion.
Actions within epsilon utility of the statewise optimum.
A prespecified class of models with near-optimal predictive performance.
c85_mapping: analogy for candidate multiplicity, not an empirical C84 geometry claim

standard_definition: Multiple similarly performing models can make different predictions or decisions.
Select test examples to label for sample-efficient model evaluation with design correction.
Allocate a budget of model probes to identify the best model.
c85_mapping: related but observation structure differs from C85 labels

standard_definition: Choose observations or allocations to optimize a registered inferential objective.
Effective number associated with a diversity order; order two is inverse squared mass.
